notes:
Like many others have mentioned, add "bBackgroundMouse=1" to the control section of your Fallout4.ini in <SteamDir>/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini

title: Fallout 4

notes:
Tweaks:
Ini files can be found here: ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/

Fix Mouse added bBackgroundMouse=1 to Fallout4.ini
Fix Horizontal line in certain lighting change bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=1 to bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0

#1 Install winetricks 
sudo apt-get install winetricks 

#2 Install xact 
WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx winetricks --force xact 

#3 Run wine config 
WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx winecfg 

#4 Create xaudio overrides Click on the Libraries tab to create overrides for the properties xaudio2_6 and xaudio2_7 and set them both to native

title: Fallout 4

notes:
1) Add "bBackgroundMouse=1" to "Controls" section in file: ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini
======================================================================================
2)  Add "bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0" to "Display" section in same file
======================================================================================
3) Run in bash console (if you use proton 3.16):
==================bash=shell===============================================================
export WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx;
export WINE=$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16/dist/bin/wine;
winetricks --force xact;
$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16/dist/bin/wine winecfg
==================end=================================================================
in config window set libraries "xaudio2_6" and "xaudio2_7" to native.

title: Fallout 4

notes: I had to add "bBackgroundMouse=1" to the "Controls" part of Fallout4.ini. You may also have to add it to the Fallout4Custom.ini. I also had to disable ESync, and tell Proton to use an emulated desktop at my resolution of 1920x1080. Configure that for your resolution. I added native overrides for xaudio2_6 and xaudio2_7. With those fixes, I'm able to run Fallout 4 on maximum with no issue.
title: Fallout 4

notes: After tweaking Fallout.ini in  the correct <appid>/pfx WINEPREFIX and adding native override for xaudio2_6 and xaudio 2_7, installing xact via wintricks, the game still showed a black screen i needed to add PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% to launcher options!!!!! That did the trick - runs perfectly smooth without any hazzle

notes:
did xaudio2_6 and xaudio2_7 in winecfg (had no audio after initial splash screen), added bBackgroundMouse=1 to Fallout4.ini as my mouse was very sluggish.

game suddenly froze and died after ~1/2h of playing and wouldn't restart. updated to latest proton beta, and it black-screened (and on the wrong monitor).

title: Fallout 4

notes:
Did the same as these instructions: (thanks to the original post)
1-Add "bBackgroundMouse=1" to "Controls" section in file: ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout4/Fallout4.ini 

2- Add "bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0" to "Display" section in same file 

3- Run in bash console : 
$ export WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx 
$ export WINE=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16/dist/bin/wine 
$ winetricks --force xact 
$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16/dist/bin/wine winecfg in config window set libraries "xaudio2_6" and "xaudio2_7" to native. 

But also added in to use a emulated desktop at my resolution of 1920x1080 which fixed my issue where my mouse would scroll left but only so far right.

4- Steam launch options: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%

Now seems to run perfectly.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
Launcher works and game starts without any modifications. Initially I could not get character dialogue audio to work, but after following the tweaks described by previous posters I am able to play the game without many issues; the one thing I did different is install the latest "winetricks" from the github repo as opposed to my distros' package repo, which is incredibly outdated. 
There are minor issues with the mouse where at times it doesn't allow movement beyond a certain range, but that goes away by switching weapons. Sometimes the game also hangs when I exit and I have to forcibly kill the application.
title: Fallout 4

notes: winetricks xact; winecfg to add x_audio2_6 and x_audio2_7
title: Fallout 4

notes: Had some severe graphic glitches at first, but those are now sorted out (by adding "bBackgroundMouse=1" and "bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0" to Fallout4.ini and disabling esync). Audio is partially missing (could be xaudio, haven't looked into it as of now) and the game freezes completely when it saves upon closing, quicksaves/loads work fine though.
title: Fallout 4

notes: Add bBackgroundMouse=1 and tile tweak to Fallout4.ini, run WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx winetricks xact, run WINEPREFIX=$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377160/pfx wine winecfg. Audio works, no graphical issues, steady 60FPS at 900p at lower settings.
title: Fallout 4

notes:
Game requires turning off any secondary monitors (I have some xrandr "fast dials" for this)... Other your mouse cursor will escape to another monitor, in game! Also requires: mousewarpoverride="force" (see: https://wiki.winehq.org/Useful_Registry_Keys).
1-Add "bBackgroundMouse=1" to "Controls" section in file: Fallout4.ini
2- Add "bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0" to "Display" section in same file 
3- protontricks 377160 xact (then override xaudio 2.6 & xaudio 2.7 to native)

The intro videos play, the game performance is good and there is no noticeable graphical anomalies. Controls work OK (with mouse set to "background"), both keyboard and mouse.

Proton 3-16.4 does not have the latest ffmpeg patch I suspect. Leads to a lot of crackly audio - hence the low rating for the game.

Outside of Proton, with wine-staging-3.19+ffmpeg 4.0 and DXVK 0.92 + Nvidia 396.54.09 drivers, the game is very playable. Sadly Proton hasn't quite caught up with this goodness yet...
title: Fallout 4

notes:
As reported by others, only needed to add native dll xaudio2_6 and xaudio2_7, to get ingame audio working after setting up the wineprefix.   
  I also had to change the "bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0" to fallout4prefs.ini  to get rid of the issue regarding the line that travels across the center of the camera when lighting changes.

the only things not accessible is creation club content and mods(they're greyed out)., but otherwise the game seems to hold 55-60fps and occasionally it'll go to 62.  Xbox One controller seems to work perfectly fine, i merely had to turn up look sensitivity.  have not tested with a mouse, as that was buggy even under regular wine.  it could easily get gold, but the above kept it just out of reach.    Interestingly it seems to autodetect my CC content I copied over from my windows install.  I added the invalidate older files line to fallout.ini because I intend to use mods at some point, so that might have played a part in that content getting detected.

title: Fallout 4

notes:
Fixes used:

#######
Fallout.ini:
#######
bBackgroundMouse=1 in Controls
bComputeShaderDeferredTiledLighting=0 in Display

####
wine:
####
winetricks xact 
winecfg xaudio2_6, xaudio2_7 to native
winecfg a virtual desktop for multi monitor to prevent the mouse from leaving the window

###########
launch options:
###########
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=100 %command%
(the PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC fixed crackly audio i was getting and can be adjusted)

notes: After following all the instructions and additional fixes mentioned here and elsewhere (xaudio to native, mouse fixes, etc.), I was able to get audio working for a while. However, after several hours of game play, the sound issues came back. Basically, the background music and ambiance is the only sound that works, and there is no character dialogue or in-game sound such as foot steps, gun shots, etc. After restarting the game, everything works  again for a while, but after some time of play it all breaks again. Very frustrating and not worth the problem, especially since exiting the game involves doing a hard kill of the game process. I will instead wait for a more complete solution to the sound problems before trying to continue playing.

title: Fallout 4

title: Fallout 4

notes:
Needed to make some of the changes in this github comment: [https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/308?_pjax=%23js-repo-pjax-container#issuecomment-439692009.]=============================================
1) Had an issue with the camera stopping rotation caused by multi-monitor setup. I had to run winecfg and set up to run Fallout 4 in a virtual desktop and capture the mouse. This works perfectly on the left most monitor. To play on my right monitor I currently need to disable the left monitor as a workaround.==================================
2) Needed to add the pulse_latency startup option to fix audio getting stuck in a loop e.g. constant minigun firing.===============================================
3) Added the no_esync startup option to fix loading screens, but this isn't 100% fixed which is the only thing preventing a gold rating.==================================
4) I had already added bBackgroundMouse=1 to Fallout4Custom.ini, haven't tested without.
=================================================================
Otherwise good performance, no stutter, no graphical bugs, no physics issues. I haven't tested mods yet.
title: Fallout 4

notes: The game needs some tweaks to run. See https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/308 / After applying the tweaks I had to enable emulating a virtual desktop via winetricks or the mouse wouldn't be captured properly in a multimonitor setup, plus I had to disable godrays to get rid of a graphical glitch. Some very small issues remain, but none have any gameplay effects: for example sometimes TAB gets "stuck" (solution: bind the pip-boy key to something else, for everything that explicitly requires the TAB key use your mouse to click the appropriate action onscreen or, in the rare cases where that's not an option, mash ALT+TAB) and there's a minor reflection screen with workshop boundaries and water. Other than that, the game is fully playable from start to finish (and postgame too, of course!). Mods also work out of the box, the only ones that don't require the script extender (a wine patch is available for that).
title: Fallout 4

notes:
First off, kudos to the Proton team for their amazing work! With tweaks, this game (with all DLCs and few dozen mods from Steam/Steam Workshop) runs well for me. Here's what I did: 

1) Followed instructions to install Winetricks, force Xact, update configs on https://steamcommunity.com/app/377160/discussions/0/3220528325725350733/

2) Added the following launch options:
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=100 %command%

Minor issues:

You'll need to exit to menu first, then desktop. Otherwise you have to ALT-F4 to force quit Wine. Audio is not 100% stable and goes out once in awhile.  In my case it is pretty rare, once every few hours. I just exit the game and go back in again. I wish Vortex, or some other mod manager, would run natively on Linux. Setting up a Windows virtual machine, running the mod manager there and rsync to Data folder kind of defeats the purpose of running on Linux.

title: Fallout 4

notes: Previously I played almost 50 hours using the xact winecfg xaudio2_6, xaudio2_7 but the audio was still breaking often, than I tried the Faudio WMA support and it seems to fix the Audio issue for good! Just run "git clone git://github.com/FNA-XNA/FAudio.git && cd FAudio && mkdir build && cd build && cmake .. -DXNASONG=OFF -DFFMPEG=ON && make" and than copy libFAudio.so inside the folder "/dist/lib64/" of your proton installation (eg. <Your Steam Dir>/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64). If you have used any xaudio trick before you need to start with a fresh wine prefix (delete <Your Steam Dir>/steamapps/compatdata/377160 but first backup your saves!). Another very important thing: remove any launcher option for the game on Steam, if you have any.

notes: xaudio2_7 DLL override fixes audio. bBackgroundMouse=1 in [Controls] section of Fallout4.ini alleviates mouse problems partially; better with gamepad

title: Fallout 4
notes: After xact override fix, only ambient noise and some effects, no dialogue. bBackgroundMouse=1 fix does work, although at times it can restrict FOV and can be sluggish.

title: Fallout 4
notes: Requires : xaudio2_7 override (native,builtin) via winecfg. Modification of Fallout4.ini with lutris installer settings. I played 6 hours with Ultra settings, no glitches nor crashes.
